jokes.text: Pride: Make every room a bathroom.
jokes.setup: Reading a commercial tagline for bladder control pads
jokes.punchline: The grotesquely inappropriate slogan suggesting incontinence everywhere
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crafted corporate slogan parody - absurd but plausible enough to be horrifying

jokes.text: Boy meets girl. Girl gets kidnapped by asian dictator. Boy makes movie to get girl back. Girl's so grateful she does birthday things to boy. Boy falls asleep.
jokes.setup: Jack describing his love story as a 'classic'
jokes.punchline: The increasingly bizarre and inappropriate plot summary ending with anti-climactic exhaustion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating absurdism perfectly integrated with Jack's character - each beat gets more wrong

jokes.text: 'cause I'm gonna be mitt rom-mummy. I call it. You can't steal it.
jokes.setup: Liz mentioning Halloween costume ideas in March
jokes.punchline: Proudly announcing terrible pun costume and claiming ownership
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment - proud of mediocre wordplay, perfectly timed delivery

jokes.text: Why wouldn't you be mitt-zombie? Because I'm an idiot!
jokes.setup: Jack suggesting obvious better pun
jokes.punchline: Liz immediately realizing and admitting her stupidity
jokes.explanation: Perfect rapid-fire comedy timing - the immediate self-awareness is both funny and very Liz

jokes.text: You're forgetting boy meets girl's mother. They are creepily attracted to each other.
jokes.setup: Adding to the love story formula
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning inappropriate attraction to mother-in-law
jokes.explanation: Sets up the Diana subplot while being characteristically inappropriate for Jack

jokes.text: I need to thank my co-writers, gary greasy And jesus, a homeless man I stole the melody from.
jokes.setup: Jenna fantasizing about accepting an award
jokes.punchline: Casual admission to stealing from a homeless man named Jesus
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Jenna's narcissistic obliviousness - the casual criminality is beautifully absurd

jokes.text: ♪ trapper keeper, snickers bar, my thermos hit the floor ♪ ♪ broken knapsack
jokes.setup: Weird Al parodying Jenna's dramatic love song
jokes.punchline: Turning romantic lyrics into mundane school supply inventory
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody concept - the bathetic drop from romance to school supplies is perfectly executed

jokes.text: Like, when people wanna see your boobs at mardi gras.
jokes.setup: Trying to explain why Weird Al parodies are an honor
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate and nonsensical comparison
jokes.explanation: Pure Tracy logic - connecting unrelated concepts in his unique way. Character-driven humor.

jokes.text: Weird al corrupted something beautiful That was written to distract gary greasy While he came down from meth.
jokes.setup: Jenna describing her song as 'something beautiful'
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was written to distract someone during drug withdrawal
jokes.explanation: Masterful Jenna moment - the contrast between 'beautiful' and 'meth withdrawal distraction' is perfect

jokes.text: My father wore this to his high school prom. The theme was enchantment under the jim crow laws.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining his tuxedo's history
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing horrifically racist prom theme
jokes.explanation: Shocking dark comedy delivered with Kenneth's innocent obliviousness - the juxtaposition is perfect

jokes.text: [dubbed over] beautiful geniuses --Holes.
jokes.setup: Kenneth about to insult sound department
jokes.punchline: Sound department censors him in real time by dubbing over his voice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke where the sound department literally demonstrates their power mid-sentence

jokes.text: Put a message in a bottle and throw it in the river. That's where I go every day to fish for shoes.
jokes.setup: Kenneth asking for future job contact
jokes.punchline: Revealing his sad daily routine of fishing for shoes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth absurdism - the matter-of-fact delivery of impossible poverty is both funny and sad

jokes.text: Throw your resume away on the fourth floor And we'll be in touch.
jokes.setup: Kenneth asking about maintenance jobs
jokes.punchline: Janitor giving literally backwards hiring advice
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - the backwards corporate process is believably ridiculous

jokes.text: Pride: The world is your toilet.
jokes.setup: Second Pride Pad slogan reading
jokes.punchline: Even more grotesque version of the original slogan
jokes.explanation: Effective callback that escalates the original premise - makes the product even more horrifying

jokes.text: We smash cut to a night of passion In a completely dark room. Bathing suit areas get a workout.
jokes.setup: Liz writing a romantic scene for the script
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly clinical description of sex
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz awkwardness - 'bathing suit areas get a workout' is hilariously prudish

jokes.text: [ridiculous boston accent] - jack, who's calling ya At such a wicked late hour? Ya mother?
jokes.setup: Liz reading Nancy's dialogue in script
jokes.punchline: Comically exaggerated Boston accent
jokes.explanation: Standard accent comedy elevated by Liz's complete commitment to the terrible impression

jokes.text: Jack donaghy, 50s, big irish head
jokes.setup: Liz describing Jack in the screenplay
jokes.punchline: The unflattering physical description
jokes.explanation: Simple character humor. The specific phrase 'big irish head' is memorably blunt.

jokes.text: think courteney cox-- Dances with a handsome pilot-- Think a young fred grandy.
jokes.setup: Describing characters in the movie
jokes.punchline: Comparing someone to obscure Love Boat actor Fred Grandy
jokes.explanation: Obscure reference humor. Fred Grandy is an odd choice for 'handsome pilot' comparison.

jokes.text: No, that's how people from boston sound to me.
jokes.setup: Being asked if the accent is too much
jokes.punchline: Liz genuinely thinking this is realistic
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Liz's cultural obliviousness is perfectly in character

jokes.text: I got four inches of steel that might change your mind.
jokes.setup: Actor claiming to be married to a woman
jokes.punchline: Ambiguous statement that could be weapon or anatomy
jokes.explanation: Lazy innuendo that relies on juvenile double entendre without character or context

jokes.text: I turned down intercourse with harvey weinstein On no less than three occasions...Out of five.
jokes.setup: Jenna claiming fearlessness in show business
jokes.punchline: Revealing she actually said yes to Harvey Weinstein twice
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly timed comedy - the math reveals Jenna's compromised principles while staying in character

jokes.text: What do you think phil collins Was trying to do with sussudio? Which weird al famously parodied soup soupy-o.
jokes.setup: Discussion about unparodiable songs
jokes.punchline: Claiming Phil Collins tried to be unparodiable but failed, with made-up parody title
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - treating nonsense song as strategic failure is clever

jokes.text: What do you think phil collins Was trying to do with sussudio?
jokes.setup: Discussing unparodiable songs
jokes.punchline: Phil Collins as example of anti-parody songwriting
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference. 'Sussudio' as already-weird song is a good observation.

jokes.text: I'm glad to see the endgame of feminism Is women dressing like dennis the menace at work.
jokes.setup: Meeting Liz for the first time
jokes.punchline: Devastating critique of Liz's professional appearance
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly constructed insult - connects feminism, fashion, and cartoon characters in one devastating line

jokes.text: Because it's crass, jack. Like personalized wedding vows or standing up in a pool.
jokes.setup: Diana explaining why she disapproves of the TV movie
jokes.punchline: Equating it with bizarrely specific social transgressions
jokes.explanation: Perfect character establishment - Diana's arbitrary class judgments are hilariously specific

jokes.text: Like personalized wedding vows or standing up in a pool.
jokes.setup: Diana calling the TV movie crass
jokes.punchline: Her specific examples of tackiness
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Diana's uppercrust sensibilities. 'Standing up in a pool' is oddly specific.

jokes.text: My housekeeper started screaming.
jokes.setup: Describing how she found out about the movie
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of domestic staff having emotional breakdowns
jokes.explanation: Great character detail - the casual mention of staff trauma reveals Diana's detachment

jokes.text: I flew here, but I saw a train from the window.
jokes.setup: Claiming the train was disgusting
jokes.punchline: Revealing she never actually took a train
jokes.explanation: Perfect Diana moment - the illogical complaint followed by absurd justification

jokes.text: I flew here, but I saw a train from the window.
jokes.setup: Diana saying the train was disgusting
jokes.punchline: Revealing she only saw a train, didn't ride it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Diana character moment. Her snobbishness is so extreme she's disgusted by trains she only glimpsed.

jokes.text: All my life, I have been the third wheel That prevents people from having sex.
jokes.setup: Liz refusing to leave Jack and Diana alone
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission of being a chronic cockblock
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware Liz moment - the proud acceptance of her saboteur role is perfect

jokes.text: In college, they called me 'the blocker'.
jokes.setup: Continuing the cockblock theme
jokes.punchline: Revealing her official college nickname
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the previous joke - 'the blocker' becomes a recurring motif

jokes.text: I'm sorry, but you're stressed and you're lonely And that woman has flawless skin, Like an organic chicken. I haven't had lunch.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining why Jack needs supervision
jokes.punchline: Comparing Diana to food because she's hungry
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character writing - Liz's hunger derailing her protective speech is perfectly timed

jokes.text: Since the creation of earth 800 years ago.
jokes.setup: Kenneth praising NBC as the greatest company
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing his belief in young earth creationism with wrong date
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth weirdness - the casual delivery of impossible timeline is beautifully absurd

jokes.text: You must crumble like greek statues.
jokes.setup: Diana mentioning Jessup women have terrible osteoporosis
jokes.punchline: Jack's poetic but inappropriate comparison
jokes.explanation: Good Jack moment - inappropriately romantic description of bone disease

jokes.text: One in four americans has an std. Chlamydia, herpes, garden variety genital swelling-- Rectal fissures.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to break romantic tension with 'factoids'
jokes.punchline: Increasingly graphic and inappropriate medical details
jokes.explanation: Perfect blocker behavior - Liz's desperation leads to increasingly gross interruptions

jokes.text: Diana, are you a time-traveler? Because I don't know how my mother-in-law Could be younger than my wife.
jokes.setup: Lance meeting Diana for the first time
jokes.punchline: Overly complicated math-based compliment
jokes.explanation: Decent actor cliche - the overly smooth line delivered by method actor

jokes.text: I would never use that much math In complimenting a woman. Their brains can't handle it.
jokes.setup: Critiquing Lance's compliment technique
jokes.punchline: Jack's casual misogyny
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's 1950s attitudes toward women

jokes.text: The fighter pilot wrists, the hair like a mature otter's pelt, Shoulders you could ride bareback.
jokes.setup: Diana commenting on Lance's resemblance to Jack
jokes.punchline: Increasingly sexual and animal-based descriptions
jokes.explanation: Good escalating description - the shift from professional to animal to sexual is well-crafted

jokes.text: Slip what? Drill him against a wall. Wouldn't mind giving him the old skin flute.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to explain women's attraction to Lance
jokes.punchline: Increasingly crude sexual euphemisms
jokes.explanation: Awkward Liz moment but relies too heavily on crude innuendo without much cleverness

jokes.text: Don't eat that pie, don't eat my fries, don't wear that tie, Don't smell that guy.
jokes.setup: Tracy mocking potential Weird Al parodies
jokes.punchline: Absurd rhyming commands ending with 'don't smell that guy'
jokes.explanation: Decent escalating absurdism - the final command is unexpected and weird

jokes.text: I'm sorry, did you just say baloney thighs? Because that's the name of the boat weird al's gonna buy With the money he makes off you...Dumb bitch!
jokes.setup: Tracy mishearing Jenna's lyrics
jokes.punchline: Elaborate insult scenario ending with jarring name-calling
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy rant - the specific boat name and sudden apology work well together

jokes.text: There's nothing you can change pizza to. Because it's already weird.
jokes.setup: Trying to find unparodiable song topics
jokes.punchline: Realizing pizza is inherently weird
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logical realization - pizza as inherently absurd concept is clever insight

jokes.text: This ice tea is so strong.
jokes.setup: Lance method acting as Jack
jokes.punchline: Dramatic overreaction to normal beverage
jokes.explanation: Good method actor satire - the unnecessary drama over mundane things

jokes.text: Prius is the latin word for impotence.
jokes.setup: Diana asking Lance's opinion on hybrid cars as Jack
jokes.punchline: Fake etymology insulting Prius owners
jokes.explanation: Good fake Jack line - the fake Latin is perfectly pretentious and mean

jokes.text: There hasn't been a good Non-boat painting in 5,000 years.
jokes.setup: Lance staying in Jack character
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific art criticism about boats
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist art opinion - the arbitrary boat requirement is perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: Sometimes to prevent monkey business, we must create it. The blocker.
jokes.setup: Liz's strategy to prevent Jack and Diana romance
jokes.punchline: Self-identification with superhero-like title
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that elevates the nickname to mission statement - Liz owns her role

jokes.text: How is taking your mother-in-law Out on a date acting like you?
jokes.setup: Jack telling Lance to stop acting like him
jokes.punchline: Lance accidentally revealing Jack's own inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic logic - Lance's question reveals the absurdity of Jack's objection

jokes.text: It's impossible not to have a great time At planet hollywood.
jokes.setup: Lance and Diana going out for drinks
jokes.punchline: Choosing the least classy restaurant possible
jokes.explanation: Good class humor - Planet Hollywood as romantic destination is perfectly wrong

jokes.text: An idea that's off the charts, kemosabe.
jokes.setup: Jack insisting his movie needs a catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Terrible catchphrase attempt using outdated reference
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Jack's attempt at cool catchphrase is perfectly out of touch

jokes.text: Nothing can stop me now. [squeals] It's okay. I'm doing great. Everything's gonna work out.
jokes.setup: Kenneth confident about his future
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets attacked by rats while maintaining optimism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment - the instant karma followed by delusional optimism

jokes.text: ♪ I eat pizza, I eat cheese ♪ ♪ I eat lots of broccolis ♪ ♪ fart so loud, fart so loud ♪ ♪ yum-yums make me fart so loud ♪
jokes.setup: Jenna's strategy to write unparodiable song about food
jokes.punchline: Hilariously crude and childish lyrics performed seriously
jokes.explanation: Brilliant execution of absurd premise - Jenna performing toilet humor as high art

jokes.text: Oh, mr. Subhas, your wife is on the phone. She's making dinner and she wants to know If you found any prosciutto today.
jokes.setup: Kenneth now working as janitor
jokes.punchline: Addressing the head of NBC as if he's also looking for deli meat
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth obliviousness - treating the powerful executive like a regular person

jokes.text: Why are you dressed like a janitor? - It's my new job. - But you just got promoted. - Well, I've had a few setbacks
jokes.setup: Kenneth's career regression
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of going from executive to janitor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth optimism in face of total failure

jokes.text: Medically, it's a neck ridge.
jokes.setup: Being told he keeps his chin up
jokes.punchline: Literal medical correction about his anatomy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth weirdness - the medical precision applied to motivational speak

jokes.text: [whispering] I lie to myself. Every morning, when I wake up, I say, 'everything's gonna be okay.' But I'm lying. And I don't know how much longer I can do it.
jokes.setup: Kenneth sharing his secret for staying positive
jokes.punchline: Devastating revelation about his psychological survival mechanism
jokes.explanation: Masterful dark comedy - Kenneth's cheerfulness revealed as conscious delusion is heartbreaking and hilarious

jokes.text: ♪ daddy's fightin' overseas ♪ ♪ lord, keep him safe for mommy, please ♪ ♪ heart so proud ♪ ♪ daddy make my heart so proud ♪
jokes.setup: Weird Al's response to Jenna's deliberately crude song
jokes.punchline: Transforming toilet humor into heartfelt military tribute
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal - Weird Al defeats them by going sincere instead of weird, perfect meta-comedy

jokes.text: Yankovic! He reversed the parody. He normal al'd us.
jokes.setup: Realizing Weird Al's counter-strategy
jokes.punchline: 'Normal Al' as the ultimate parody move
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy - 'Normal Al' is perfect coinage for the reverse strategy

jokes.text: You know he was stationed at pearl harbor? During the korean war?
jokes.setup: Jenna talking about calling her dad
jokes.punchline: Her complete confusion about military history
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna stupidity moment. Mixing up Pearl Harbor and Korean War is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Our love is off the charts, kemosabe.
jokes.setup: Final scene of the terrible movie
jokes.punchline: Using Jack's awful catchphrase in romantic climax
jokes.explanation: Good callback that makes the terrible catchphrase even worse in romantic context

jokes.text: She made me boink her this morning And at first, I was like, 'please-- She boinks like a dog.
jokes.setup: Lance sharing inappropriate details about Diana
jokes.punchline: Crude oversharing with animal comparison
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe but relies on crude shock value - character appropriate but not clever

jokes.text: This is a group process, lemon. Don't be an egomaniac.
jokes.setup: Liz asking for first credit on the movie
jokes.punchline: Jack calling her an egomaniac for wanting credit on her own work
jokes.explanation: Good Jack hypocrisy - the irony of calling the actual writer an egomaniac
